contract.name: v2/autocomplete/accounts/epoa.md
contract.description: Autocomplete for Treasury account Ending Period of Availability (EPOA) values used in advanced search filters.

filters.description: Optional object of Treasury Account components. When omitted, null, or not an object the service returns the default top EPOA values.
filters.constraints: Recognized keys: ata, aid, bpoa, epoa, a, main, sub. Each must be a text value or null. Unknown keys are ignored. Text that fails validation (wrong type or empty for epoa) yields 400/422 errors, while otherwise-invalid structures fall back to default results.

ata.description: Allocation Transfer Agency Identifier (3 characters). Matches exactly when provided.
ata.constraints: Optional; non-text values rejected. Non-matching strings simply return no results.

aid.description: Agency Identifier (3 characters). Matches exactly when combined with other components.
aid.constraints: Optional; must be text. Numeric types return 400. Null is accepted but produced no matches in probes.

bpoa.description: Beginning Period of Availability (4 characters). Filters to accounts with the specified period.
bpoa.constraints: Optional; accepts text. Non-matching strings yield empty results.

epoa.description: Ending Period of Availability (typically 4 digits). Performs prefix matching on the provided text.
epoa.constraints: Optional string with minimum length 1; empty string returns 422. Null returns accounts missing EPOA. Arrays or other non-text values return 400.

a.constraints: Optional; text value 'X' surfaced null EPOA entries.

main.constraints: Optional text filter; combines with other components.

sub.constraints: Optional text filter; combines with other components.

limit.description: Maximum number of autocomplete values to return.
limit.constraints: Optional; defaults to 10. Accepts integer values from 0 through 500 inclusive. Digit-only strings, including those with leading zeros, are coerced. Null, booleans, decimals (even as strings), and integers above 500 return validation errors. Negative integers trigger a 500 HTML error.

results.description: Array of EPOA values as strings; elements may be null for accounts without an ending period. Observed responses were ascending and capped by the chosen limit.

quirks: `filters` is documented as required but the service accepts missing, null, or non-object values and returns default results.
Unknown keys inside `filters` are silently ignored instead of raising validation errors.
`limit` accepts digit-only strings (including leading zeros) but rejects decimals, booleans, and null; values above 500 return 422 while negative integers cause a 500 HTML error.
Responses may include `null` EPOA entries when matching accounts without an ending period.
Non-object bodies (for example an array) are treated as empty filters and succeed.
risks: Negative `limit` values trigger an unstructured 500 HTML error; clients should validate limits before calling.
Invalid filter structures are ignored, which can hide client bugs by returning broad default results.
`results` can contain `null` entries; consumers must handle missing EPOA values.
gaps: Unclear whether additional EPOA values exist beyond the 36 observed or if pagination is ever needed.

probes.notes: pass – prefix filter with limit 5 returned ascending EPOA values.
fail – docs require filters but empty body returned default results.
pass – default limit of 10 applied when limit omitted.
pass – limit 0 returned an empty array.
fail – negative limit triggered a 500 HTML error.
pass – digit-only string limit with leading zero was accepted.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'limit'. '5.0' is not a valid type (integer)"}

probes.notes: fail – decimal string rejected as non-integer input.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'limit'. 'None' is not a valid type (integer)"}

probes.notes: fail – null limit rejected as wrong type.
pass – empty epoa string failed minimum length validation.
pass – null epoa returned records with missing ending period.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'filters|epoa'. '[]' is not a valid type (text)"}

probes.notes: fail – array assigned to epoa triggered type validation error.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'filters|aid'. '123' is not a valid type (text)"}

probes.notes: pass – numeric aid rejected as wrong type.
fail – null filters treated as empty filters despite documentation.

mismatches: Documentation states `filters` is required, but the endpoint returns data when it is omitted, null, or non-object.
Documentation omits the enforced maximum `limit` of 500; values above that return 422.
Documentation treats `limit` as a generic number, yet the service requires integers and rejects floats while coercing digit-only strings.
gaps: Unclear whether additional EPOA values exist beyond the 36 observed or if pagination is ever needed.
risks: Negative `limit` values trigger an unstructured 500 HTML error; clients should validate limits before calling.
Invalid filter structures are ignored, which can hide client bugs by returning broad default results.
`results` can contain `null` entries; consumers must handle missing EPOA values.
